chunk_summary: This section outlines a stepwise, risk-stratified approach to managing pediatric obesity and screening for comorbidities in school and community settings, emphasizing non-stigmatizing, family-centered interventions and school-based health promotion strategies.
learning_objectives: Understand the stepwise approach to managing pediatric overweight and obesity in community settings.
Identify key comorbidities associated with pediatric obesity and appropriate screening methods.
Learn strategies to integrate school-based health promotion activities into obesity management.
Recognize the importance of non-stigmatizing, family-centered counseling in pediatric obesity care.
key_facts: BMI-for-age is a recommended tool for identifying excess adiposity in children.

For overweight children without comorbidities, prioritize behavior and environment changes such as reducing sugary beverages and ultra-processed snacks, increasing physical activity, and limiting screen time.
For children with obesity, especially those over 10 years, comorbidity screening should include blood pressure measurement and metabolic risk assessment.
School environments are critical leverage points for promoting healthy behaviors, including healthy canteen policies and regular physical education.
Adolescents benefit from interventions targeting sleep regularity, stress management, and food environments, with a focus on health and function outcomes rather than appearance.
reference_quote: Identify excess adiposity using body mass index (BMI)-for-age where feasible (school health screening is a high-yield entry point).
